opus: noun. A work of music or set of works with a specified rank in an ordering of a composer's complete published works.
ors: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orang Seletar.
pious: adjective. Of or pertaining to piety, exhibiting piety, devout, god-fearing.
pis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pijin.
piso: noun. Alternative form of peso (“currency of Philippines”).
piu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pintupi-Luritja.
pour: verb. To cause (liquid, or liquid-like substance) to flow in a stream, either out of a container or into it.
pours: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of pour
psi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeast Pashayi.
pur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Puruborá.
puri: noun. A type of unleavened bread from India and Pakistan, usually deep-fried.
pus: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Pashto.
rip: verb. To divide or separate the parts of (especially something flimsy, such as paper or fabric), by cutting or tearing; to tear off or out
sip: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sikkimese.
sir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siri.
sis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siuslaw.
sop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Songe.
sorus: noun. Any reproductive structure, in some lichens and fungi, that produces spores.
sos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seenku.
sou: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Thai.
spur: noun. A rigid implement, often roughly y-shaped, that is fixed to one's heel for the purpose of prodding a horse.
spurious: adjective. False, not authentic, not genuine.
sri: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siriano.

usurps: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of usurp
